attachment.content:
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

usage: hsim estimate [-h] [--slice SLICE] [--icount ICOUNT] [--max-k MAX_K]
                     [--vsim-icount VSIM_ICOUNT] [--mmap-size MMAP_SIZE]
                     elf

End-to-end SimPoint cycle estimator. Runs an M4 BBV profile pass, clusters the
result with SimPoint, drains QEMU + vsim for each cluster representative
slice, and aggregates a weighted CPI to produce an estimated cycle count:
estimated_cycles = N_total * sum_i(w_i * CPI_i). Composes F9 (profile +
cluster) and F11 (slice mode) end to end.

positional arguments:
  elf                   path to a RISC-V ELF (relative or absolute)

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --slice SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 1000)
  --icount ICOUNT       total retired insns for the M4 profile pass (default:
                        10000)
  --max-k MAX_K         SimPoint -maxK (default: 2; tiny BBVs need
                        conservative K)
  --vsim-icount VSIM_ICOUNT
                        vsim --hybrid-icount per phase (default: 0 -> use
                        --slice for SimPoint-correct CPI math)
  --mmap-size MMAP_SIZE
                        shared mmap size for the profile + per-phase drain
                        pairs (default: 128M; accepts SPEC like 8G, 512M,
                        1024). Set HSIM_MMAP_DIR=/path to redirect off
                        /dev/shm.

file.content:
# --- F12: estimate (SimPoint cycle aggregator) -----------------------------

# End-to-end: M4 profile (F9) -> SimPoint cluster (F9) -> per-phase
# QEMU+vsim drain (mirrors --mode slice from F11 but keeps state files
# long enough to read mcycle). Aggregator math:
#     CPI_i = delta_mcycle_i / vsim_icount
#     CPI   = sum_i ( w_i * CPI_i )
#     cycles_estimate = N_total_insns * CPI


def _read_state_mcycle(state_path: Path) -> int:
    """Read the mcycle field from a hybrid_state_v1 file via orchestrator.abi.

    Lazy import keeps `./hsim list` etc. from paying the ctypes layout
    check at startup.
    """
    tools_dir = str(REPO_ROOT / "tools")
    if tools_dir not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, tools_dir)
    from orchestrator.abi import HybridStateV1
    state = HybridStateV1.from_buffer_copy(state_path.read_bytes())
    return int(state.mcycle)


def _estimate_one_phase(elf: Path, artifacts: dict[str, Path],
                        slice_n: int, slice_at: int,
                        qemu_icount_fallback: int,
                        vsim_icount: int,
                        mmap_size: int) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """Run one slice-consume QEMU + vsim drain pair, return mcycle pair.

    Mirrors _run_drain_pair (--mode slice in cmd_run) but keeps the
    state files long enough to read mcycle from both ends. Raises
    RuntimeError on drain rc != 200; caller handles cleanup of any
    partial output.
    """
    label = f"phase{slice_at}"
    shm_path = _load_hsim_shm().allocate(
        mmap_size, prefix=f"hsim-{label}-shm.",
    )
    state_q_fd, state_q_name = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=f"hsim-{label}-qstate.")
    os.close(state_q_fd)
    state_q = Path(state_q_name)
    state_v_fd, state_v_name = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=f"hsim-{label}-vstate.")
    os.close(state_v_fd)
    state_v = Path(state_v_name)
    try:
        qemu_argv = _qemu_drain_argv(
            artifacts["qemu"], elf, artifacts["plugin"],
            shm_path, mmap_size, state_q,
            plugin_extras=(
                f"slice={slice_n}",
                f"slice_at={slice_at}",
                f"icount={qemu_icount_fallback}",
            ),
        )
        proc = subprocess.run(
            qemu_argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
            timeout=_PROFILE_TIMEOUT_S,
        )
        if proc.returncode != _QEMU_DRAIN_RC:
            tail = proc.stdout.decode("utf-8", "replace").splitlines()[-10:]
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"QEMU phase slice_at={slice_at} expected exit "
                f"{_QEMU_DRAIN_RC}, got {proc.returncode}: "
                + "; ".join(tail)
            )
        mcycle_before = _read_state_mcycle(state_q)

        vsim_argv = _vsim_drain_argv(
            artifacts["vsim"], elf, state_q, state_v, vsim_icount,
        )
        proc = subprocess.run(
            vsim_argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
            timeout=_VSIM_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_S,
        )
        if proc.returncode != _QEMU_DRAIN_RC:
            tail = proc.stdout.decode("utf-8", "replace").splitlines()[-10:]
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"vsim phase slice_at={slice_at} expected exit "
                f"{_QEMU_DRAIN_RC}, got {proc.returncode}: "
                + "; ".join(tail)
            )
        mcycle_after = _read_state_mcycle(state_v)
        return mcycle_before, mcycle_after
    finally:
        for p in (shm_path, state_q, state_v):
            try:
                p.unlink()
            except FileNotFoundError:
                pass


def cmd_estimate(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    elf = Path(args.elf)
    if not elf.is_absolute():
        elf = (Path.cwd() / elf).resolve()
    if not elf.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: ELF not found: {args.elf}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
    for label, path in artifacts.items():
        if not path.is_file():
            print(f"hsim: {label} not built -- run ./hsim build",
                  file=sys.stderr)
            return 2
    if not _SIMPOINT_BIN.is_file():
        print("hsim: simpoint not built -- run ./hsim build",
              file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    # SimPoint-correct default: vsim simulates exactly one slice's
    # insns per phase, so CPI_i = delta_mcycle / slice. 0 = "auto".
    vsim_icount = args.vsim_icount if args.vsim_icount > 0 else args.slice

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="hsim-estimate-") as tmpdir:
        tmp = Path(tmpdir)

file.content:
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="hsim-estimate-") as tmpdir:
        tmp = Path(tmpdir)
        bbv = tmp / "profile.bb"
        simpts = tmp / "simpts"
        weights = tmp / "weights"

        rc = cmd_profile(argparse.Namespace(
            elf=str(elf), slice=args.slice, icount=args.icount,
            output=str(bbv), dry_run=False,
            mmap_size=args.mmap_size,
        ))
        if rc != 0:
            return rc

        rc = cmd_cluster(argparse.Namespace(
            bbv=str(bbv), max_k=args.max_k,
            simpts=str(simpts), weights=str(weights), dry_run=False,
        ))
        if rc != 0:
            return rc

        slice_indices = [int(line.split()[0])
                         for line in simpts.read_text().splitlines()
                         if line.strip()]
        weight_vals = [float(line.split()[0])
                       for line in weights.read_text().splitlines()
                       if line.strip()]
        if len(slice_indices) != len(weight_vals):
            print(f"hsim: simpts/weights row mismatch "
                  f"({len(slice_indices)} vs {len(weight_vals)})",
                  file=sys.stderr)
            return 1

        phases = []
        for K, w in zip(slice_indices, weight_vals):
            print(f"[hsim estimate] phase slice_at={K} weight={w:.3f}",
                  flush=True)
            try:
                mc_before, mc_after = _estimate_one_phase(
                    elf, artifacts, args.slice, K,
                    args.icount, vsim_icount,
                    mmap_size=args.mmap_size,
                )
            except RuntimeError as exc:
                print(f"hsim: estimate failed: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
                return 1
            delta = mc_after - mc_before
            cpi = delta / vsim_icount if vsim_icount > 0 else 0.0
            phases.append({"K": K, "w": w, "delta": delta, "cpi": cpi})

        weighted_cpi = sum(p["w"] * p["cpi"] for p in phases)
        estimated_cycles = int(args.icount * weighted_cpi)

        print(f"M4 profile: {len(slice_indices)} BBV rows ({bbv.name})")
        print(f"cluster: {len(phases)} phases")
        for p in phases:
            print(f"phase K={p['K']} w={p['w']:.3f} "
                  f"delta_mcycle={p['delta']} CPI={p['cpi']:.3f}")
        print(f"estimated_cycles={estimated_cycles}  "
              f"(N={args.icount}, weighted_CPI={weighted_cpi:.3f})")
        return 0


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )

content.text:
`./hsim estimate` is a 3-stage SimPoint-style cycle estimator. It samples behavior with fast QEMU, picks representative chunks with SimPoint, runs only those chunks on cycle-accurate vsim, then projects.

### Stage 1 - profile (fast, QEMU only)
- QEMU runs your ELF for `--icount` total retired insns (default 10000).
- A TCG plugin chops execution into slices of `--slice` insns (default 1000) and emits one **BBV row** per slice. A BBV (Basic Block Vector) is a histogram: which basic blocks executed in this slice, and how many times.
- For your run: 10000 / 1000 = 10 BBV rows written to `profile.bb`.

### Stage 2 - cluster (SimPoint binary, no simulation)
- The `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint` tool reads the BBVs and groups slices that look behaviorally similar (k-means in BBV space).
- Output: a list of **representative slice indices** (`simpts`) and **weights** (`weights`) that sum to 1.0.
- For your run with `--max-k 2`: 2 phases.
  - K=0  w=0.400 -> the `init()` phase (slice 0..1000, simple store loop)
  - K=4  w=0.600 -> the `gemm()` inner kernel (slice 4000..5000, FP multiply-add chain)

### Stage 3 - per-phase drain pair (cycle-accurate vsim)
For each representative slice K:
1. **QEMU drain**: re-run QEMU with `slice_at=K`. QEMU runs forward to insn K*slice, drains full arch state (GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs) to a `hybrid_state_v1` file, exits with code 200. Read `mcycle` -> `mc_before`.
2. **vsim drain**: vsim resumes from that state file, executes exactly `--vsim-icount` insns (default = `--slice`, so 1000), drains state back, exits 200. Read `mcycle` -> `mc_after`.
3. `delta_mcycle = mc_after - mc_before` = real cycles vsim spent on that phase's 1000 insns.

### The math
```
CPI_i           = delta_mcycle_i / vsim_icount
weighted_CPI    = Σ_i ( w_i × CPI_i )
estimated_cycles = N_total × weighted_CPI         (N_total = --icount)
```

Your numbers:
- phase 0: 1585 cyc / 1000 insn -> CPI 1.585 (init: simple stores)
- phase 4: 9769 cyc / 1000 insn -> CPI 9.769 (GEMM: FP MAC dependency chain stalls the pipeline)
- weighted_CPI = 0.4×1.585 + 0.6×9.769 = 6.495
- estimated_cycles = 10000 × 6.495 = **64954**

### Why this is useful
- Profile pass: ~MHz (QEMU). Cycle-accurate vsim pass: ~kHz, but only on K×slice = 2000 insns instead of all 10000. Speedup grows with `--icount`/`(K×slice)`.
- The 64954 is *projected* cycles for 10000 insns. To estimate the **full** program, raise `--icount` to the real retire count, or interpret `weighted_CPI` (6.5) as cycles-per-insn for the workload's steady-state mix.

### Code map
- `hsim:1027` `cmd_estimate` - orchestrator
- `hsim:725` `cmd_profile` - F9 stage 1 (calls QEMU + BBV plugin)
- `hsim:794` `cmd_cluster` - F9 stage 2 (calls `simpoint` binary)
- `hsim:956` `_estimate_one_phase` - F11 drain pair per phase
- `hsim:942` `_read_state_mcycle` - parses `mcycle` out of the `hybrid_state_v1` file via `tools/orchestrator/abi.py`

message.content: at 1. QEMU drain: Read mcycle -> mc_before  ? read from where? qemu or vsim?

input.description: Find mcycle handling in QEMU plugin

content.text:
**Both come from `mcycle`, but read at different points by different components.** They're both extracted from the `hybrid_state_v1` binary file -- one written by QEMU, one written by vsim.

```
                                        state_q file              state_v file
                                        (QEMU wrote it)           (vsim wrote it)
   QEMU drain ──[plugin reads QEMU's mcycle]──▶ mcycle=X
                                                  │
                                                  ▼
   vsim ◀─[ResumeDriver writes mcycle=X into hart via DMI]
   vsim runs vsim_icount insns; RTL increments mcycle in cycle-accurate land
   vsim drain ──[StateDrain reads hart's mcycle via DMI]──▶ mcycle=Y
                                                                │
                                                                ▼
   hsim reads mcycle field from state_q → mc_before = X
   hsim reads mcycle field from state_v → mc_after  = Y
   delta = Y - X = real RTL cycles spent in vsim
```

Concretely in the code:
- `hsim:1000` `mcycle_before = _read_state_mcycle(state_q)` -- reads from the file **QEMU wrote** (qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:263 dumped QEMU's `mcycle` reg into it).
- `hsim:1017` `mcycle_after = _read_state_mcycle(state_v)` -- reads from the file **vsim wrote** (state_drain.hpp:80 read the hart's `mcycle` CSR via DMI).

### Why subtracting them is meaningful
QEMU's mcycle is **not** cycle-accurate (QEMU is functional). But the absolute value doesn't matter -- what matters is that vsim **continues counting from that same value**:

1. `resume_driver.hpp:118` writes `state.mcycle` (= QEMU's X) into vsim's hart.
2. Vsim's RTL increments mcycle by **real cycles** per tick.
3. `Y - X` cancels the QEMU starting value, leaving only the cycles vsim itself consumed.

So `delta_mcycle` is the cycle-accurate cost of the slice, regardless of what nonsense QEMU's mcycle baseline was.

message.content: explain --icount total retired insns? how user decides it?

content.text:
`--icount` is the **total number of retired RISC-V instructions QEMU executes during the profile pass**. It serves two roles:

### Role 1 - bound for the profile pass
QEMU starts the ELF and stops after `--icount` insns. The TCG plugin emits one BBV row every `--slice` insns, so:

```
num_BBV_rows = icount / slice
```

Your run: `--icount 10000 --slice 1000` -> 10 BBV rows.

### Role 2 - N_total in the projection formula
```
estimated_cycles = icount × weighted_CPI
```
So `--icount` is **the workload size you're asking the tool to estimate cycles for**. The tool doesn't know your "real" program length -- it just projects from the sampled CPI to whatever N you pass.

### How to pick a value

| Goal | Pick `--icount` as |
|------|--------------------|
| Cycles for the **whole program** | Real retire count of a full QEMU run. Set `--icount` >= that; QEMU exits before it caps if the program finishes. |
| Cycles for a **hot region of N insns** | N |
| Quick CPI characterization (don't care about the cycle number, just want `weighted_CPI`) | Anything >= `2 × max_k × slice` so SimPoint has enough rows to cluster |

**Minimum rule**: `icount / slice >= 2 × max_k`. With `--max-k 2` you need >= 4 BBV rows -> `--icount >= 4 × slice`. Default 10000 / 1000 = 10 rows, comfortably above.

### To get your GEMM's real total
Run QEMU without the icount cap, count retired insns, then pass that as `--icount`. Quick way:

```sh
$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc -O2 -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d \
   gemm.c -lgloss_vh -o gemm.elf

./build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
   -machine virt -bios none -kernel gemm.elf \
   -plugin ./QEMU/build/contrib/plugins/libinsn.so,inline=on -d plugin 2>&1 | grep insns
```

Or simpler: just pass `--icount 200000` (your 32^3 GEMM is ~80-120K insns plus startup) and `--slice 2000`. The profile pass exits when the program ends anyway. The resulting `estimated_cycles` then approximates the full program.

### Tradeoff in one line
Bigger `--icount` -> longer (still MHz-fast) QEMU profile pass and finer-grained BBV, but **vsim cost stays at K × slice insns** because vsim only runs the cluster reps. That's the whole point of SimPoint.

input.description: Check for exit-time hook in plugin

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text:
**Short answer**: not today. The plugin only drains on an explicit trigger (icount, slice-at, CSR, or PC). If the program returns from `main` before any trigger fires, the profile pass exits 0 (program exit code) instead of 200 (drain), and `cmd_profile` rejects it.

But the fix is small. Two practical options:

### Option A - just oversize `--icount`
The plugin caps the run. If you pick `--icount` >= real total, you'll always hit the cap mid-program (never see natural exit), so it works today. Trade-off: you don't measure the tail.

```sh
./hsim estimate gemm.elf --icount 1000000 --slice 5000 --max-k 4
```

A 32x32x32 GEMM is well under 1M insns. This is the **pragmatic answer right now**.

### Option B - add `--icount 0` = "run to program exit"
Real fix, ~30 lines. Two pieces:

1. **Plugin**: register a `qemu_plugin_register_atexit_cb` that runs the same drain path on natural exit. The drained state's `minstret` field carries the real total retired insns.
2. **hsim**: when `--icount 0`, skip the plugin `icount=` arg, accept exit-code 200 from atexit, and after `cmd_profile` reads `N_total = state.minstret` from the profile-pass state file. Pass that as the multiplier in the estimate formula.

UX after the change:

```sh
./hsim estimate gemm.elf --slice 5000      # runs the whole program, no icount knob
```

### Recommendation
Use Option A today (pick a big icount). If you want me to implement Option B, I'd:
1. Add the atexit drain hook to `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`
2. Make `--icount` default to `0` in `cmd_estimate`
3. Read `minstret` from the profile-pass state file as `N_total`
4. Update `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`

Per CLAUDE.md (TDD) I'd write the failing test first: an e2e test that runs `./hsim estimate gemm.elf` with no `--icount` and expects exit 0 + `estimated_cycles=` output. Want me to go ahead?
I'll follow TDD: read existing code/tests first, then write the failing test, then implement.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# RED test for F12: `./hsim estimate <elf> --slice N --icount M --max-k K`.
3	# End-to-end SimPoint cycle estimator: runs M4 profile + cluster +
4	# per-phase QEMU/vsim drain, aggregates weighted CPI -> estimated_cycles.
5	#
6	# Workload: icount_spin.elf. The full pipeline takes ~30-60s
7	# (profile + cluster + 2 phase drains). Cap at 120s for headroom.
8	
9	set -uo pipefail
10	
11	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
12	ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
13	HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"
14	
15	fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
16	ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }
17	
18	[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"
19	ELF="$ROOT/tests/fixtures/icount_spin.elf"
20	[ -f "$ELF" ] || fail "missing fixture: $ELF (run ./hsim build first)"
21	
22	cd "$ROOT"
23	
24	# 1. estimate --help mentions the key flags.
25	out=$("$HSIM" estimate --help 2>&1) || fail "estimate --help non-zero: $out"
26	for flag in --slice --icount --max-k --vsim-icount; do
27	  echo "$out" | grep -qE -- "$flag" \
28	    || fail "estimate --help missing $flag:"$'\n'"$out"
29	done
30	ok "estimate --help mentions --slice, --icount, --max-k, --vsim-icount"
31	
32	# 2. estimate with no ELF -> rc=2 (argparse required positional).
33	set +e
34	out=$("$HSIM" estimate 2>&1)
35	rc=$?
36	set -e
37	[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "estimate (no arg) rc=$rc (expected 2):"$'\n'"$out"
38	ok "estimate without ELF -> rc=2"
39	
40	# 3. Bogus ELF -> rc=2 with hint.
41	set +e
42	out=$("$HSIM" estimate "/no/such/foo.elf" 2>&1)
43	rc=$?
44	set -e
45	[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "estimate bogus rc=$rc (expected 2):"$'\n'"$out"
46	echo "$out" | grep -qiE 'not found|no such' \
47	  || fail "estimate bogus missing 'not found' hint:"$'\n'"$out"
48	ok "estimate bogus ELF -> rc=2 with hint"
49	
50	# 4. Missing vsim artifact -> rc=2 with 'not built' hint.
51	set +e
52	out=$(VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim "$HSIM" estimate "$ELF" 2>&1)
53	rc=$?
54	set -e
55	[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "missing-vsim estimate rc=$rc (expected 2):"$'\n'"$out"
56	echo "$out" | grep -qE 'vsim.*not built' \
57	  || fail "missing-vsim no 'not built' hint:"$'\n'"$out"
58	ok "missing vsim artifact -> rc=2 with 'not built' hint"
59	
60	# 5. Real estimate run on icount_spin produces estimated_cycles line.
61	set +e
62	out=$(timeout 120 "$HSIM" estimate "$ELF" \
63	        --slice 1000 --icount 10000 --max-k 2 2>&1)
64	rc=$?
65	set -e
66	[ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || fail "estimate real run rc=$rc:"$'\n'"$out"
67	echo "$out" | grep -qE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' \
68	  || fail "missing 'estimated_cycles=<int>' line:"$'\n'"$out"
69	# Parse the estimated cycle count and assert > 0.
70	EST=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' | head -1 \
71	        | cut -d= -f2)
72	[ "${EST:-0}" -gt 0 ] \
73	  || fail "estimated_cycles must be > 0, got '$EST':"$'\n'"$out"
74	ok "estimate emits estimated_cycles=$EST"
75	
76	# 6. Per-phase rows show delta_mcycle values.
77	echo "$out" | grep -qE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' \
78	  || fail "missing per-phase 'delta_mcycle=<int>' rows:"$'\n'"$out"
79	N_PHASES=$(echo "$out" | grep -cE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+')
80	[ "$N_PHASES" -ge 1 ] \
81	  || fail "need at least 1 per-phase row, got $N_PHASES:"$'\n'"$out"
82	# Each delta_mcycle should be > 0 (vsim ran insns, mcycle advanced).
83	while IFS= read -r delta; do
84	  [ "$delta" -gt 0 ] \
85	    || fail "delta_mcycle must be > 0, got '$delta':"$'\n'"$out"
86	done < <(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2)
87	ok "$N_PHASES per-phase row(s) with delta_mcycle > 0"
88	
89	echo "PASS test_hsim_estimate"
90

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F12: `./hsim estimate <elf> --slice N --icount M --max-k K`.
# End-to-end SimPoint cycle estimator: runs M4 profile + cluster +
# per-phase QEMU/vsim drain, aggregates weighted CPI -> estimated_cycles.
#
# Workload: icount_spin.elf. The full pipeline takes ~30-60s
# (profile + cluster + 2 phase drains). Cap at 120s for headroom.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"
ELF="$ROOT/tests/fixtures/icount_spin.elf"
[ -f "$ELF" ] || fail "missing fixture: $ELF (run ./hsim build first)"

cd "$ROOT"

# 1. estimate --help mentions the key flags.
out=$("$HSIM" estimate --help 2>&1) || fail "estimate --help non-zero: $out"
for flag in --slice --icount --max-k --vsim-icount; do
  echo "$out" | grep -qE -- "$flag" \
    || fail "estimate --help missing $flag:"$'\n'"$out"
done
ok "estimate --help mentions --slice, --icount, --max-k, --vsim-icount"

# 2. estimate with no ELF -> rc=2 (argparse required positional).
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" estimate 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "estimate (no arg) rc=$rc (expected 2):"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate without ELF -> rc=2"

# 3. Bogus ELF -> rc=2 with hint.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" estimate "/no/such/foo.elf" 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "estimate bogus rc=$rc (expected 2):"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qiE 'not found|no such' \
  || fail "estimate bogus missing 'not found' hint:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate bogus ELF -> rc=2 with hint"

# 4. Missing vsim artifact -> rc=2 with 'not built' hint.
set +e
out=$(VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim "$HSIM" estimate "$ELF" 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "missing-vsim estimate rc=$rc (expected 2):"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'vsim.*not built' \
  || fail "missing-vsim no 'not built' hint:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "missing vsim artifact -> rc=2 with 'not built' hint"

# 5. Real estimate run on icount_spin produces estimated_cycles line.
set +e
out=$(timeout 120 "$HSIM" estimate "$ELF" \
        --slice 1000 --icount 10000 --max-k 2 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || fail "estimate real run rc=$rc:"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'estimated_cycles=<int>' line:"$'\n'"$out"
# Parse the estimated cycle count and assert > 0.
EST=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' | head -1 \
        | cut -d= -f2)
[ "${EST:-0}" -gt 0 ] \
  || fail "estimated_cycles must be > 0, got '$EST':"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate emits estimated_cycles=$EST"

# 6. Per-phase rows show delta_mcycle values.
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing per-phase 'delta_mcycle=<int>' rows:"$'\n'"$out"
N_PHASES=$(echo "$out" | grep -cE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+')
[ "$N_PHASES" -ge 1 ] \
  || fail "need at least 1 per-phase row, got $N_PHASES:"$'\n'"$out"
# Each delta_mcycle should be > 0 (vsim ran insns, mcycle advanced).
while IFS= read -r delta; do
  [ "$delta" -gt 0 ] \
    || fail "delta_mcycle must be > 0, got '$delta':"$'\n'"$out"
done < <(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2)
ok "$N_PHASES per-phase row(s) with delta_mcycle > 0"

echo "PASS test_hsim_estimate"

1	# ROADMAP: `./hsim` end-user driver
2	
3	Tracks the rollout of a single, end-user-friendly entry point
4	(`./hsim`) that hides the developer-grade plumbing currently exposed
5	by `setup.sh`, `tests/e2e.sh`, and `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.
6	
7	Plan source-of-truth: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md`.
8	
9	One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress)
10	and on completion (done). Each feature is its own commit (or a small
11	structural-then-behavioral pair), never mixed.
12	
13	## Status Legend
14	
15	- not-started: feature has not begun
16	- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
17	- done: RED test was written, GREEN landed, and the feature's done-criteria pass
18	
19	## Global Invariant
20	
21	After every feature lands, this must hold:
22	
23	```
24	JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
25	# pass list == 45 PASS / 0 FAIL  (baseline from e2e_ROADMAP.md F0)
26	```
27	
28	`./hsim` only WRAPS the existing scripts; it MUST NOT change their
29	behavior. Any divergence from the e2e baseline blocks the next feature.
30	
31	## Dependency DAG
32	
33	```
34	F1 (list) --> F2 (build) --> F3 (run) --> F4 (test) --> F5 (docs) --> F6 (verify)
35	                                                            |
36	                                                            +-- can also land any time after F4
37	                                                            +-- F7 (doctor)             [post-F6, reuses F3/F4 helpers]
38	                                                            +-- F8 (demo)               [post-F6, reuses F3 helpers]
39	                                                            +-- F9 (profile + cluster)  [post-F6, reuses F3 helpers]
40	                                                            +-- F10 (archive + replay)  [post-F6, reuses F3 helpers]
41	                                                            +-- F11 (run --mode icount/slice) [post-F9, reuses _qemu_drain_argv]
42	                                                            +-- F12 (estimate)          [post-F9+F11, composes profile/cluster/slice]
43	                                                            +-- F13 (mmap-size + HSIM_MMAP_DIR) [post-F12, factors out allocate() helper]
44	```
45	
46	Strict serial order F1 -> F4 because each later feature reuses the
47	`Paths` / `run_subprocess` helpers introduced by the earlier ones.
48	F5 (docs) may land between F4 and F6 or after F6. F7 (doctor) reuses
49	`_resolve_artifacts` (F3) and `_DEFAULT_JOBS` (F4); F8 (demo) reuses
50	`_resolve_artifacts` (F3); F9 (profile/cluster) also reuses
51	`_resolve_artifacts` (F3); F10 (archive/replay) reuses
52	`_resolve_artifacts` (F3) and imports `orchestrator.checkpoint`
53	in-process; F11 (run --mode icount/slice) extends F3's `cmd_run`
54	and reuses `_qemu_drain_argv` introduced by the F9->F11 structural
55	refactor; F12 (estimate) composes F9's `cmd_profile` + `cmd_cluster`
56	with a slice-mode drain pair and reads mcycle via
57	`orchestrator.abi.HybridStateV1`; F13 (mmap-size + HSIM_MMAP_DIR)
58	extracts the three duplicated mkstemp+truncate alloc sites into
59	`tools/hsim_shm.allocate()` and adds a `--mmap-size SPEC` flag plus
60	env-driven backend dir. None of these have a runtime dependency on
61	the e2e path, so they can land any time after F6.
62	
63	## Pre-flight decisions (locked at plan time)
64	
65	1. **Entry point name** -- `./hsim` at repo root (executable Python script).
66	2. **Language** -- Python 3, stdlib only (no pip deps).
67	3. **Run scope** -- accepts both fixture-by-name and arbitrary ELF paths.
68	4. **Missing artifacts** -- fail with one-line hint, no auto-build.
69	5. **Trigger mode** -- explicit `--mode csr|pc` required in v1.
70	6. **Output style** -- quiet by default, `--verbose` flag exists.
71	7. **Out of scope for v1** -- `demo`, `doctor`, auto-detect mode, M3..M8.
72	
73	## Features
74	
75	### F1 - `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`
76	
77	- Status: done
78	- Type: behavioral (new code)
79	- Description: Lowest-risk subcommand -- pure parsing, no subprocess.
80	  Lets the user discover fixture names without grepping the Makefile.
81	- Key files:
82	  - **Create:** `hsim` (Python 3 script, ~80 lines for this feature only).
83	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` (RED test).
84	- Dependencies: none.
85	- Done criteria:
86	  - `./hsim list fixtures` exits 0 and outputs `rt_c_hello` and `rt_all_gprs`.
87	  - `./hsim list demos` exits 0 and outputs `demo_csr_oneshot` and `demo_pc_oneshot`.
88	  - `./hsim list modes` exits 0 and outputs `M1` and `M8`.
89	  - `./hsim list bogus` exits 2 with a usage hint.
90	  - The RED test in `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` is green.
91	
92	### F2 - `./hsim build [--what TARGET]`
93	
94	- Status: done
95	- Type: behavioral
96	- Description: One entry point for all artifact builds. Wraps
97	  `setup.sh` (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint) and
98	  `make -C tests/fixtures` (ELF fixtures). No reimplementation -- the
99	  subprocess delegates to the existing scripts.
100	- Key files:
101	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `build` subparser).
102	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` (RED test with `--dry-run`).
103	- Dependencies: F1 (shares argparse skeleton + `Paths` helper).
104	- Done criteria:
105	  - `./hsim build --dry-run` prints the underlying `bash setup.sh` /
106	    `make -C tests/fixtures` commands without executing.
107	  - `./hsim build --what qemu --dry-run` prints only the QEMU build line.
108	  - `./hsim build` from a clean checkout produces all four artifacts +
109	    fixtures and exits 0.
110	  - `setup.sh` is unchanged on disk.
111	
112	### F3 - `./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc`
113	
114	- Status: done
115	- Type: behavioral
116	- Description: One-shot round-trip runner. Wraps `tests/e2e.sh` with
117	  the right env vars. Path-only contract: the positional arg is always
118	  an ELF path (relative resolves against CWD, absolute is honored).
119	  No bare-fixture-name shortcut. Quiet 3-line progress output by
120	  default; `--verbose` prefixes the resolved env + command line.
121	- Key files:
122	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `run` subparser + path-resolution helper +
123	    artifact pre-flight).
124	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` (RED test).
125	- Dependencies: F1 (Paths helper), F2 (so artifacts can be built first).
126	- Done criteria:
127	  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf` (no `--mode`) exits 2
128	    with usage hint mentioning `--mode`.
129	  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr` on a clean
130	    build exits 0 in <30s.
131	  - `./hsim run /abs/path/foo.elf --mode pc` resolves the path and runs.
132	  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr`
133	    exits 2 with `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
134	  - `--verbose` prints the resolved FIXTURE/TRIGGER_MODE/VSIM_ELF and
135	    the `bash tests/e2e.sh` command before launching it.
136	
137	### F4 - `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`
138	
139	- Status: done
140	- Type: behavioral
141	- Description: Wraps `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with the JOBS=8 cap pre-set
142	  (per project memory for the 128-core host). Parses the script's
143	  `results.tsv` and prints a summary line `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z`.
144	  On failure, prints the per-test log paths.
145	- Key files:
146	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `test` subparser, results.tsv parser).
147	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_test_small.sh` (RED test that
148	    runs `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr`).
149	- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3.
150	- Done criteria:
151	  - `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 and
152	    prints `PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0` (plus elapsed time).
153	  - `./hsim test` (no args) caps at JOBS=8 by default.
154	  - `./hsim test --jobs 4` overrides the cap.
155	  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` is unchanged on disk.
156	
157	### F5 - Docs: README + USER_GUIDE + CLAUDE.md
158	
159	- Status: done
160	- Type: structural (no code change; doc edits)
161	- Description: Make `./hsim` the canonical entry point in every
162	  user-visible doc. The dev-facing env-var surface stays documented
163	  but is demoted to "escape hatch" sections.
164	- Key files:
165	  - **Modify:** `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh`
166	    quickstart block (lines 12-22) with `./hsim` quickstart.
167	  - **Modify:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new
168	    "Section 0.5: Quick start with `./hsim`" before the existing
169	    Section 1 build instructions. Keep Sections 1-7 unchanged.
170	  - **Modify:** `CLAUDE.md` -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build`
171	    and `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark raw scripts as
172	    developer escape hatch.
173	- Dependencies: F1..F4 (so the examples in docs work).
174	- Done criteria:
175	  - README quickstart is 3 lines and uses only `./hsim` commands.
176	  - USER_GUIDE Section 0.5 has a copy-pastable 4-command worked
177	    example.
178	  - CLAUDE.md still lists the underlying scripts for developers but
179	    points to `./hsim` first.
180	
181	### F6 - End-to-end verification
182	
183	- Status: done
184	- Type: no commit (safety net)
185	- Description: Run the 11-step smoke sequence from the plan against a
186	  clean checkout. Tick each step.
187	- Steps:
188	  ```
189	  ./hsim                                                          # help
190	  ./hsim list fixtures                                            # ~17 fixtures
191	  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr             # exit 2 if no build
192	  ./hsim build                                                    # ~5 min cold
193	  ./hsim list fixtures                                            # unchanged
194	  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr             # PASS ~6s
195	  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr -v          # verbose
196	  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_all_gprs.elf --mode csr            # arbitrary path
197	  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf --mode pc             # PC trigger
198	  ./hsim test --filter rt_c --mode csr                            # ~7 tests
199	  ./hsim test                                                     # full suite
200	  ```
201	- Dependencies: F1..F5.
202	- Done criteria: all 11 steps exit as expected; the full-suite line
203	  reports the same 45 PASS / 0 FAIL as the e2e baseline.
204	
205	### F7 - `./hsim doctor`
206	
207	- Status: done
208	- Type: behavioral (new code + RED test)
209	- Description: Read-only env-health diagnoser. Prints four sections
210	  (artifacts, submodules, toolchain, runtime), each row tagged
211	  PASS / FAIL / WARN / INFO. Exit code = 1 if any FAIL row, else 0.
212	  Reuses `_resolve_artifacts` (F3) and `_DEFAULT_JOBS` (F4); does not
213	  touch `tests/e2e.sh` or `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.
214	- Key files:
215	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `_doctor_*` helpers, `cmd_doctor`,
216	    `p_doctor` subparser).
217	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_doctor.sh` (RED test, 6
218	    assertions).
219	- Dependencies: F3 (artifact resolver), F4 (JOBS constant).
220	- Done criteria:
221	  - `./hsim doctor` on the built repo exits 0 and prints the four
222	    section headers plus a `Summary:` line.
223	  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim ./hsim doctor` exits 1 with a FAIL row
224	    mentioning `vsim`.
225	  - `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` is honored from the env or parsed from
226	    `config.env` (no hardcoded toolchain path).
227	  - Doctor completes in <=5 s (no subprocess shell-outs to QEMU/vsim).
228	  - The RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_doctor.sh` is green.
229	
230	### F8 - `./hsim demo <T1..T7|short-name> [-- KEY=VAL ...]`
231	
232	- Status: done
233	- Type: behavioral (new code + RED test)
234	- Description: One subcommand wraps the seven demo scripts under
235	  `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`. Selector accepts three forms:
236	  - T-id: `T1..T7` (mirrors `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`).
237	  - Short name: `csr_oneshot`, `simpoint_loop`, etc.
238	  - Full filename stem: `demo_csr_oneshot` (copy-paste from
239	    `./hsim list demos`).
240	  Passthrough `-- KEY=VAL ...` injects per-run env overrides (e.g.
241	  `./hsim demo T7 -- SLICE=400 MAXK=4`) so users can tune SLICE,
242	  MAXK, etc. without bypassing the wrapper.
243	- Key files:
244	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `_DEMO_ORDER`, `_resolve_demo`,
245	    `_parse_demo_passthrough`, `cmd_demo`, `p_demo` subparser;
246	    split `argv` on first `--` in `main()` so argparse never sees
247	    the passthrough tail).
248	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_demo.sh` (RED test, 7
249	    assertions).
250	- Dependencies: F3 (`_resolve_artifacts` for pre-flight).
251	- Done criteria:
252	  - `./hsim demo --help` lists the T1..T7 mapping.
253	  - `./hsim demo` (no selector) exits 2 with usage hint.
254	  - `./hsim demo bogus` exits 2 with `unknown demo` hint.
255	  - `./hsim demo T1 --dry-run` prints
256	    `bash .../tests/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`.
257	  - `./hsim demo csr_oneshot --dry-run` resolves to the same
258	    script (short-name form).
259	  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim ./hsim demo T1` exits 2 with the
260	    `vsim not built -- run ./hsim build` hint (mirrors `./hsim run`).
261	  - `./hsim demo T7 --dry-run -- SLICE=400 MAXK=4` shows
262	    `SLICE=400` + `MAXK=4` in the resolved env.
263	  - The RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_demo.sh` is green.
264	
265	### F9 - `./hsim profile <elf>` + `./hsim cluster <bbv>` (M4 pair)
266	
267	- Status: done
268	- Type: behavioral (new code + RED test)
269	- Description: First-class subcommands for the M4 BBV profile +
270	  SimPoint clustering steps. Two commands because the output of
271	  the first is the input of the second; users can inspect the
272	  BBV row count between them.
273	  - `./hsim profile <elf> --slice N --icount M -o BBV` runs one
274	    QEMU+plugin pass with `bbv_out=,slice=N,icount=M` and writes
275	    a SimPoint-compatible `.bb` file.
276	  - `./hsim cluster <bbv> --max-k K --simpts P --weights P`
277	    shells to `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint` and writes per-cluster
278	    slice indices + weights.
279	  Plumbing: `profile` inlines the QEMU argv (mirrors
280	  `orchestrator.spawn.qemu_drain_argv` plus the M4 plugin extras),
281	  manages a `/dev/shm`-backed mmap via `tempfile.mkstemp` +
282	  `os.truncate`, expects QEMU exit 200 (drain). `cluster` uses
283	  `-dim 2` to match the demo; widen to a flag if larger fixtures
284	  need it later. Pre-flight: `profile` checks qemu + plugin only
285	  (vsim is not invoked); `cluster` checks the SimPoint binary.
286	- Key files:
287	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `_qemu_profile_argv`, `cmd_profile`,
288	    `cmd_cluster`, `p_profile`, `p_cluster` subparsers, `_SIMPOINT_BIN`
289	    constant).
290	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_profile_cluster.sh` (RED test,
291	    9 assertions).
292	- Dependencies: F3 (`_resolve_artifacts`).
293	- Done criteria:
294	  - `./hsim profile --help` mentions `--slice`, `--icount`, `-o`.
295	  - `./hsim cluster --help` mentions `--max-k`, `--simpts`, `--weights`.
296	  - `./hsim profile` with no ELF -> rc=2 with usage hint.
297	  - `./hsim profile /no/such.elf` -> rc=2 with `not found` hint.
298	  - `./hsim profile <elf> --dry-run` prints the resolved QEMU cmd
299	    and does NOT write the output file.

301	    `qemu not built` hint.
302	  - `./hsim profile tests/fixtures/icount_spin.elf --slice 1000 --icount 10000 -o /tmp/foo.bb` exits 0 and writes 10 BBV rows.
303	  - `./hsim cluster /no/such.bb` -> rc=2 with `not found` hint.
304	  - `./hsim cluster /tmp/foo.bb --max-k 2 --simpts S --weights W` exits 0 with non-empty outputs.
305	  - The RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_profile_cluster.sh` is green.
306	  - `JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` still 45 PASS / 0 FAIL.
307	
308	### F10 - `./hsim archive` + `./hsim replay` (M6 + M7 pair)
309	
310	- Status: done
311	- Type: behavioral (new code + RED test)
312	- Description: First-class subcommands for M6 (checkpoint archive)
313	  and M7 (checkpoint replay). Two commands because the output of the
314	  first is the input of the second; users can inspect or relocate
315	  the snap directory between them.
316	  - `./hsim archive --state S --mmap M --elf E -o snap/N` imports
317	    `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive` in-process and writes
318	    `snap/N/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}`. `--mmap-size` defaults to
319	    128 MiB (matches every fixture and demo).
320	  - `./hsim replay snap/N --resume-with {vsim,qemu}` spawns
321	    `python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint` with
322	    `PYTHONPATH=tools/` and the resolved hybrid artifacts.
323	  Plumbing rationale: archive runs in-process because hsim and the
324	  orchestrator are both Python (subprocess just adds ~100 ms startup
325	  and obscures tracebacks); replay shells out because the resume
326	  pipeline is long-running and the existing CLI already streams
327	  stdout correctly. Pre-flight: archive validates `--state` +
328	  `--mmap` files exist; replay validates the snap dir AND the three
329	  hybrid artifacts (vsim/qemu/plugin).
330	- Key files:
331	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `_load_checkpoint_module`, `cmd_archive`,
332	    `cmd_replay`, `p_archive`, `p_replay` subparsers,
333	    `_DEFAULT_MMAP_SIZE` constant).
334	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_archive_replay.sh` (RED test,
335	    11 assertions; synthesises a valid state.bin via
336	    `orchestrator.abi` so the test is self-contained).
337	- Dependencies: F3 (`_resolve_artifacts`).
338	- Done criteria:
339	  - `./hsim archive --help` mentions `--state`, `--mmap`, `--elf`, `-o`.
340	  - `./hsim replay --help` mentions `--resume-with` and the snap dir.
341	  - `./hsim archive` with no args -> rc=2 (argparse required-flag).
342	  - `./hsim archive --state /no/such ...` -> rc=2 with `not found` hint.
343	  - `./hsim archive --mmap /no/such ...` -> rc=2 with `not found` hint.
344	  - `./hsim archive ... --dry-run` prints state/mmap/elf, does NOT
345	    create the snap dir.
346	  - Real `./hsim archive` writes `state.bin + mem + meta.json`,
347	    meta.json contains the correct `flags` list from state.bin.
348	  - `./hsim replay /no/such` -> rc=2 with `not found` hint.
349	  - `./hsim replay snap/N --dry-run` prints the resolved
350	    `orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint ... --resume-with vsim` argv.
351	  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim ./hsim replay snap/N` -> rc=2 with
352	    `vsim not built` hint.
353	  - The RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_archive_replay.sh` is green.
354	  - `JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` still 45 PASS / 0 FAIL.
355	
356	### F11 - `./hsim run --mode {icount, slice}` (M3 + M5 pair)
357	
358	- Status: done
359	- Type: structural (extract `_qemu_drain_argv` helper) +
360	  behavioral (cmd_run branching + new --mode choices + new flags +
361	  RED test). Landed as two commits in TDD order: structural first
362	  (no behavior change; verified by re-running F9's RED test),
363	  behavioral second.
364	- Description: Extend F3's `cmd_run` with two new `--mode` choices
365	  for CSR-less workloads where `csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1}` markers are not
366	  available. csr/pc paths remain byte-identical (delegate to
367	  `tests/e2e.sh`); icount/slice spawn QEMU + vsim directly because
368	  `tests/e2e.sh` only recognises csr/pc triggers.
369	  - `--mode icount`: QEMU plugin `icount=N` drain -> vsim
370	    `--hybrid-icount M` drain. Flags: `--qemu-icount` (default
371	    10000), `--vsim-icount` (default 5000).
372	  - `--mode slice`: QEMU plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K,icount=fallback`
373	    drain -> vsim `--hybrid-icount M` drain. Flags: `--slice`
374	    (default 1000), `--slice-at` (default 1), plus the icount flags.
375	  Plumbing: the structural refactor extracts `_qemu_drain_argv`
376	  (with a `plugin_extras` tuple) from `_qemu_profile_argv`; both
377	  modes call it with mode-specific extras. vsim spawn uses a new
378	  `_vsim_drain_argv` helper (no `--shared-mem-*` flags, matching
379	  the demos for CSR-less workloads). Both spawns must exit 200.
380	- Key files:
381	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (split `cmd_run` into `_run_via_e2e` for
382	    csr/pc and `_run_drain_pair` for icount/slice; add
383	    `_vsim_drain_argv`, `_VSIM_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_S`; extend p_run
384	    choices + flags; refactor `_qemu_profile_argv` to call
385	    `_qemu_drain_argv`).
386	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_icount_slice.sh` (RED
387	    test, 6 assertions).
388	- Dependencies: F3 (`cmd_run`, `_resolve_artifacts`); reuses F9's
389	  `_SHM_SIZE`, `_PROFILE_TIMEOUT_S`, `_QEMU_DRAIN_RC`.
390	- Done criteria:
391	  - `./hsim run --help` lists `csr`, `pc`, `icount`, `slice` as
392	    `--mode` choices.
393	  - `./hsim run --help` mentions `--qemu-icount`, `--vsim-icount`,
394	    `--slice`, `--slice-at`.
395	  - `./hsim run /no/such --mode icount` -> rc=2 with path hint.
396	  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such ./hsim run X --mode icount` -> rc=2 with
397	    `not built` hint.
398	  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/icount_spin.elf --mode icount
399	    --qemu-icount 10000 --vsim-icount 5000` exits 0.
400	  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/icount_spin.elf --mode slice
401	    --slice 1000 --slice-at 1 --vsim-icount 5000` exits 0.
402	  - The pre-F11 RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` is
403	    still green (csr/pc path unchanged).
404	  - The RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_icount_slice.sh` is green.
405	  - `JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` still 45 PASS / 0 FAIL.
406	
407	### F12 - `./hsim estimate <elf>` (SimPoint cycle estimator)
408	
409	- Status: done
410	- Type: behavioral (new code + RED test).
411	- Description: End-to-end SimPoint cycle estimator. Composes F9
412	  (`cmd_profile` + `cmd_cluster`) with a slice-mode QEMU+vsim drain
413	  pair, reads `mcycle` at both ends, and aggregates a weighted CPI:
414	  ```
415	  CPI_i           = delta_mcycle_i / vsim_icount
416	  weighted_CPI    = sum_i ( w_i * CPI_i )
417	  estimated_cycles = N_total_insns * weighted_CPI
418	  ```
419	  Plumbing rationale:
420	  - profile / cluster reuse F9's `cmd_profile` and `cmd_cluster`
421	    by synthesizing `argparse.Namespace` and calling them directly
422	    (no subprocess re-spawn).
423	  - per-phase drain pair mirrors `_run_drain_pair` from F11 but
424	    keeps state files long enough to call `_read_state_mcycle`
425	    at both ends; light intentional duplication for surgical scope.
426	  - `_read_state_mcycle` lazy-imports `orchestrator.abi.HybridStateV1`
427	    so unrelated subcommands don't pay the ctypes layout check at
428	    startup.
429	  - default `--vsim-icount = --slice` (when user passes 0) so each
430	    phase simulates exactly one slice of insns, giving
431	    SimPoint-correct CPI math by default.
432	- Key files:
433	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `_read_state_mcycle`,
434	    `_estimate_one_phase`, `cmd_estimate`, `p_estimate` subparser).
435	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate.sh` (RED test, 6
436	    assertions).
437	- Dependencies: F9 (`cmd_profile`, `cmd_cluster`, `_SIMPOINT_BIN`),
438	  F11 (`_qemu_drain_argv`, `_vsim_drain_argv`, `_QEMU_DRAIN_RC`,
439	  `_PROFILE_TIMEOUT_S`, `_VSIM_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_S`).
440	- Done criteria:
441	  - `./hsim estimate --help` mentions `--slice`, `--icount`,
442	    `--max-k`, `--vsim-icount`.
443	  - `./hsim estimate` with no ELF -> rc=2.
444	  - `./hsim estimate /no/such` -> rc=2 with `not found` hint.
445	  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such ./hsim estimate X` -> rc=2 with `not built`
446	    hint.
447	  - `./hsim estimate tests/fixtures/icount_spin.elf --slice 1000
448	    --icount 10000 --max-k 2` exits 0; output contains
449	    `estimated_cycles=<int>` with integer > 0.
450	  - Per-phase rows show `delta_mcycle=<int>` with each delta > 0.
451	  - The RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate.sh` is green.
452	  - `JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` still 45 PASS / 0 FAIL.
453	
454	### F13 - `./hsim {run,profile,estimate} --mmap-size SPEC` + `HSIM_MMAP_DIR`
455	
456	- Type: behavioral.
457	- Description: user-facing knobs for requesting an mmap larger than
458	  `/dev/shm` capacity. Adds `--mmap-size SPEC` (suffix `K|M|G|T` or
459	  raw bytes; default `128M`) to the four subparsers that allocate
460	  shared mmaps: `run`, `profile`, `archive` (already had it as
461	  `type=int`; converted to SPEC parsing for compatibility), `estimate`.
462	  Adds `HSIM_MMAP_DIR=/path` env var to override the backing
463	  directory. Selection policy is one rule: honor the env var if set
464	  (else `/dev/shm`), validate via `os.statvfs`, error rc=2 with a
465	  clear hint if the pinned dir cannot hold `size + 256 MiB margin`.
466	  No filesystem-type probing, no fallback chain -- the user owns
467	  disk topology. Status line printed on every allocation:
468	  `[hsim mmap] 8.0 GiB at /scratch (free: 64.0 GiB)`.
469	- Plumbing:
470	  - **Create:** `tools/hsim_shm.py` -- `parse_size(spec) -> int`,
471	    `select_mmap_dir(size) -> Path`, `allocate(size, prefix) -> Path`.
472	    Single source of truth; replaces three duplicated
473	    `mkstemp(dir="/dev/shm") + os.truncate(_, _SHM_SIZE)` blocks in
474	    `hsim`.
475	  - **Modify:** `hsim` -- add `_load_hsim_shm()` lazy importer +
476	    `_parse_mmap_size(spec)` argparse wrapper. Migrate
477	    `_run_drain_pair`, `cmd_profile`, `_estimate_one_phase` to
478	    `allocate()`. Delete duplicate `_SHM_SIZE`; keep
479	    `_DEFAULT_MMAP_SIZE` as the canonical 128 MiB constant. Add
480	    `--mmap-size SPEC` to `p_run`, `p_profile`, `p_estimate`;
481	    convert `p_archive`'s `type=int` to `type=_parse_mmap_size`.
482	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_mmap_backend.sh` (RED test,
483	    8 assertions).
484	- Dependencies: F9 (`cmd_profile`), F11 (`_run_drain_pair`,
485	  `_qemu_drain_argv`), F12 (`_estimate_one_phase`, `cmd_estimate`).
486	- Done criteria:
487	  - `./hsim {run,profile,estimate} --help` all mention `--mmap-size`.
488	  - `--mmap-size 8XBG` -> rc=2 with parse-error hint.
489	  - `--mmap-size 999T` -> rc=2 with NoSpace hint.
490	  - `HSIM_MMAP_DIR=/no/such` -> rc=2 with directory hint.
491	  - `HSIM_MMAP_DIR=$tmp --mmap-size 128M --dry-run` -> rc=0 with
492	    `[hsim mmap]` status line referencing `$tmp`.
493	  - Default (no flag, no env) still uses `/dev/shm` at 128 MiB.
494	  - All F9..F12 RED tests pass byte-identically.
495	  - `JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` still 45 PASS / 0 FAIL.
496	
497	## Out of scope (deferred to a future driver_ROADMAP_v2)
498	
499	- Threading plugin params (`bbv_out=`, `slice=`, `icount=`,
500	  `slice_at=`) through `orchestrator.spawn.qemu_drain_argv` so the
501	  M3..M5 demos and `./hsim {profile,run --mode icount,run --mode
502	  slice,estimate}` share one argv builder via the orchestrator.
503	  F9 + F11 + F12 inline the argv as an interim step
504	  (`_qemu_drain_argv` in hsim itself).
505	- Bash-completion file.
506	- Multi-fixture batch mode for `./hsim estimate`.
507	- Caching of profile output across `./hsim estimate` runs.
508	- Migrating the 14 hardcoded `mktemp -p /dev/shm` shell sites
509	  (`tests/e2e.sh`, `tests/demos/*.sh`, `tests/drivers/*.sh`,
510	  `tests/plugin/*.sh`) to call `tools/hsim_shm.allocate`. F13
511	  exposes the helper but only the Python entry points use it; the
512	  shell harnesses all default to 128 MiB which always fits
513	  `/dev/shm`. Mechanical sweep when needed.
514

file.content:
# ROADMAP: `./hsim` end-user driver

Tracks the rollout of a single, end-user-friendly entry point
(`./hsim`) that hides the developer-grade plumbing currently exposed
by `setup.sh`, `tests/e2e.sh`, and `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

Plan source-of-truth: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md`.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress)
and on completion (done). Each feature is its own commit (or a small
structural-then-behavioral pair), never mixed.

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: RED test was written, GREEN landed, and the feature's done-criteria pass

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
# pass list == 45 PASS / 0 FAIL  (baseline from e2e_ROADMAP.md F0)
```

`./hsim` only WRAPS the existing scripts; it MUST NOT change their
behavior. Any divergence from the e2e baseline blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 (list) --> F2 (build) --> F3 (run) --> F4 (test) --> F5 (docs) --> F6 (verify)
                                                            |
                                                            +-- can also land any time after F4
                                                            +-- F7 (doctor)             [post-F6, reuses F3/F4 helpers]
                                                            +-- F8 (demo)               [post-F6, reuses F3 helpers]
                                                            +-- F9 (profile + cluster)  [post-F6, reuses F3 helpers]
                                                            +-- F10 (archive + replay)  [post-F6, reuses F3 helpers]
                                                            +-- F11 (run --mode icount/slice) [post-F9, reuses _qemu_drain_argv]
                                                            +-- F12 (estimate)          [post-F9+F11, composes profile/cluster/slice]
                                                            +-- F13 (mmap-size + HSIM_MMAP_DIR) [post-F12, factors out allocate() helper]
```

Strict serial order F1 -> F4 because each later feature reuses the
`Paths` / `run_subprocess` helpers introduced by the earlier ones.
F5 (docs) may land between F4 and F6 or after F6. F7 (doctor) reuses
`_resolve_artifacts` (F3) and `_DEFAULT_JOBS` (F4); F8 (demo) reuses
`_resolve_artifacts` (F3); F9 (profile/cluster) also reuses
`_resolve_artifacts` (F3); F10 (archive/replay) reuses
`_resolve_artifacts` (F3) and imports `orchestrator.checkpoint`
in-process; F11 (run --mode icount/slice) extends F3's `cmd_run`
and reuses `_qemu_drain_argv` introduced by the F9->F11 structural
refactor; F12 (estimate) composes F9's `cmd_profile` + `cmd_cluster`
with a slice-mode drain pair and reads mcycle via
`orchestrator.abi.HybridStateV1`; F13 (mmap-size + HSIM_MMAP_DIR)
extracts the three duplicated mkstemp+truncate alloc sites into
`tools/hsim_shm.allocate()` and adds a `--mmap-size SPEC` flag plus
env-driven backend dir. None of these have a runtime dependency on
the e2e path, so they can land any time after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (locked at plan time)

1. **Entry point name** -- `./hsim` at repo root (executable Python script).
2. **Language** -- Python 3, stdlib only (no pip deps).
3. **Run scope** -- accepts both fixture-by-name and arbitrary ELF paths.
4. **Missing artifacts** -- fail with one-line hint, no auto-build.
5. **Trigger mode** -- explicit `--mode csr|pc` required in v1.
6. **Output style** -- quiet by default, `--verbose` flag exists.
7. **Out of scope for v1** -- `demo`, `doctor`, auto-detect mode, M3..M8.

## Features

### F1 - `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral (new code)
- Description: Lowest-risk subcommand -- pure parsing, no subprocess.
  Lets the user discover fixture names without grepping the Makefile.
- Key files:
  - **Create:** `hsim` (Python 3 script, ~80 lines for this feature only).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: none.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim list fixtures` exits 0 and outputs `rt_c_hello` and `rt_all_gprs`.
  - `./hsim list demos` exits 0 and outputs `demo_csr_oneshot` and `demo_pc_oneshot`.
  - `./hsim list modes` exits 0 and outputs `M1` and `M8`.
  - `./hsim list bogus` exits 2 with a usage hint.
  - The RED test in `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` is green.

### F2 - `./hsim build [--what TARGET]`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One entry point for all artifact builds. Wraps
  `setup.sh` (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint) and
  `make -C tests/fixtures` (ELF fixtures). No reimplementation -- the
  subprocess delegates to the existing scripts.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `build` subparser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` (RED test with `--dry-run`).
- Dependencies: F1 (shares argparse skeleton + `Paths` helper).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim build --dry-run` prints the underlying `bash setup.sh` /
    `make -C tests/fixtures` commands without executing.
  - `./hsim build --what qemu --dry-run` prints only the QEMU build line.
  - `./hsim build` from a clean checkout produces all four artifacts +
    fixtures and exits 0.
  - `setup.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F3 - `./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One-shot round-trip runner. Wraps `tests/e2e.sh` with
  the right env vars. Path-only contract: the positional arg is always
  an ELF path (relative resolves against CWD, absolute is honored).
  No bare-fixture-name shortcut. Quiet 3-line progress output by
  default; `--verbose` prefixes the resolved env + command line.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `run` subparser + path-resolution helper +
    artifact pre-flight).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: F1 (Paths helper), F2 (so artifacts can be built first).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf` (no `--mode`) exits 2
    with usage hint mentioning `--mode`.
  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr` on a clean
    build exits 0 in <30s.
  - `./hsim run /abs/path/foo.elf --mode pc` resolves the path and runs.
  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr`
    exits 2 with `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
  - `--verbose` prints the resolved FIXTURE/TRIGGER_MODE/VSIM_ELF and
    the `bash tests/e2e.sh` command before launching it.

### F4 - `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Wraps `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with the JOBS=8 cap pre-set
  (per project memory for the 128-core host). Parses the script's
  `results.tsv` and prints a summary line `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z`.
  On failure, prints the per-test log paths.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `test` subparser, results.tsv parser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_test_small.sh` (RED test that
    runs `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr`).
- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 and
    prints `PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0` (plus elapsed time).
  - `./hsim test` (no args) caps at JOBS=8 by default.
  - `./hsim test --jobs 4` overrides the cap.
  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F5 - Docs: README + USER_GUIDE + CLAUDE.md

- Status: done
- Type: structural (no code change; doc edits)
- Description: Make `./hsim` the canonical entry point in every
  user-visible doc. The dev-facing env-var surface stays documented

- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh`

    example.

### F6 - End-to-end verification

- Status: done
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Run the 11-step smoke sequence from the plan against a
  clean checkout. Tick each step.
- Steps:
  ```
  ./hsim                                                          # help
  ./hsim list fixtures                                            # ~17 fixtures
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr             # exit 2 if no build
  ./hsim build                                                    # ~5 min cold
